# braintrustdata_score (Resource)

Manages a Braintrust project score.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "braintrustdata_project" "evaluation" {
  name        = "score-example-project"
  description = "Project for score resource examples"
}

resource "braintrustdata_score" "quality" {
  project_id  = braintrustdata_project.evaluation.id
  name        = "quality"
  score_type  = "free-form"
  description = "Free-form score for evaluator feedback"
}

output "score_id" {
  value = braintrustdata_score.quality.id
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The score name.
- `project_id` (String) The project ID that owns the score.
- `score_type` (String) The score type, such as `categorical`.

### Optional

- `categories` (String) The score categories as a JSON-encoded string.
- `config` (String) The score configuration as a JSON-encoded string.
- `description` (String) The score description.

### Read-Only

- `created` (String) The timestamp when the score was created.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the score.
- `position` (String) LexoRank position of the score within the project.
- `user_id` (String) The ID of the user who created the score.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Scores can be imported using their ID
terraform import braintrustdata_score.quality score-id-here
```
